\subsection*{Title}
Existence and Uniqueness of the Trawin Zero Point

\subsection*{Canonical Statement}
\begin{verbatim}
There exists exactly one point $p$ in the Trawinistic manifold $M$ such that for all geodesics $\gamma$ in $M$, $\lim_{\tau\to\infty}\gamma(\tau)=p$; furthermore, $p$ is the unique fixed point under any symmetry-preserving diffeomorphism $\phi: M \to M$.\\\
\end{verbatim}
